behaviors.description: Required patient fields (name, date of birth, insurance ID) are validated before any downstream processing and missing fields produce a clear error listing all missing items.
behaviors.justification: Missing-field listing itself is unit-testable, but "before any downstream processing" is a module-interaction gate (validator -> orchestrator). No real OS/network/subprocess or AI output dependence is required.
behaviors.test_description: Run intake orchestration with payload missing multiple required fields; assert returned error lists all missing fields and assert insurance/triage/scheduling/confirmation modules are not invoked.

behaviors.description: Date of birth validation rejects future dates, dates more than 150 years ago, and non-date strings.
behaviors.justification: Pure validation logic on one input field; no cross-module handoff needed to reveal this failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Call DOB validator with future date, >150-year-old date, invalid string, and valid boundary dates; assert exact acceptance/rejection results.

behaviors.description: The insurance eligibility check sends the correct patient ID and plan code to the payer API and correctly interprets `eligible` vs `not eligible` vs `pending`.
behaviors.justification: This is adapter wiring + status mapping across modules (request builder/client/mapper). Higher levels were rejected because behavior text does not require real external contract verification, only correct outbound fields and internal interpretation.
behaviors.test_description: Use a controllable fake payer endpoint/client; assert outbound payload includes correct patient ID and plan code, then feed each status and assert mapped internal eligibility state.

behaviors.description: When the payer API is unreachable, the system marks eligibility as `pending` and continues the intake rather than blocking.
behaviors.justification: Needs orchestration of error handling and continuation path. Rejected System because behavior does not require proving real network timeout semantics, only handling unreachable condition and continuing flow.
behaviors.test_description: Inject connection-failure/timeout from payer client; assert eligibility becomes pending and downstream triage/scheduling steps still run.

behaviors.description: Triage invocation includes the reported symptoms and configured emergency rubric in the model input payload.
behaviors.justification: This checks prompt/payload assembly and handoff to model client, not model judgment quality. Rejected Agentic because behavior text is only about input payload contents.
behaviors.test_description: Run triage service with known symptoms/rubric and capture model request payload; assert both fields are present and correctly formatted.

behaviors.description: The live AI symptom triage assigns high urgency to symptom combinations that indicate emergency conditions (for example chest pain plus shortness of breath).
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model judgment/quality; deterministic stubs would hide failures in urgency assignment.
behaviors.test_description: Run repeated live triage calls on emergency symptom combinations and assert urgency is high with acceptable consistency thresholds.

behaviors.description: The triage result parser stores a clinician-readable explanation field (not only a numeric score).
behaviors.justification: Focus is parser-to-storage handoff (model response parser + persistence). Rejected Agentic because behavior does not require explanation quality, only presence/storage of field.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative triage response containing score and explanation through parse/store path; assert persisted record includes non-empty explanation field.

behaviors.description: The live AI symptom triage explanation cites case-specific symptoms and urgency rationale, not generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: This is output quality/content relevance from the real model; stubs cannot validate case-specific rationale behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run live triage on multiple symptom sets; assert explanations reference case symptoms and explicit urgency rationale rather than generic templates.

behaviors.description: An appointment is scheduled in the first available slot that matches the assigned urgency level: high urgency gets same-day, medium gets within 48 hours.
behaviors.justification: Needs scheduling logic + calendar DB interaction and time-window constraints across modules. No explicit real external API/OS requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Seed calendar DB with conflicting availability; run scheduling for high and medium urgency and assert selected slot is earliest valid one in required window.

behaviors.description: The scheduling step does not double-book a time slot even when two patients are processed concurrently.
behaviors.justification: Concurrency/transaction behavior appears when scheduler and shared DB interact. Rejected System because behavior text does not require subprocess/OS-level behavior or external service round-trip.
behaviors.test_description: Execute two concurrent scheduling requests targeting same remaining slot against real test DB constraints/transactions; assert only one booking succeeds for that slot.

behaviors.description: The confirmation message includes patient name, appointment time, provider name, and preparation instructions appropriate to visit type.
behaviors.justification: Requires data assembly across intake/scheduling/provider/prep-instructions modules. Rejected System because behavior text does not require validating external delivery channel contract.
behaviors.test_description: Run confirmation generation from completed intake record and assert message contains required fields and visit-type-specific preparation instructions.

behaviors.description: Pipeline orchestration (validate, insurance, triage, schedule, confirm) completes correctly with deterministic stubbed triage output.
behaviors.justification: Explicitly a multi-module orchestration check with deterministic stubbed triage, which disqualifies Agentic/Workflow for this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end orchestration in-process with stubbed triage output and controlled insurance/scheduling dependencies; assert stage order, state handoff, and successful completion.

behaviors.description: For a single real patient submission using only the data provided in that submission, the full live intake pipeline (validate, insurance, triage, schedule, confirm) completes without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: Live triage introduces real model dependency; pipeline success depends on real AI step plus real external interactions. Rejected Workflow because only one explicitly agentic stage (triage) is described.
behaviors.test_description: Execute one real submission through production-like path with live payer call, live triage call, real scheduling write/readback, and confirmation generation; assert automated completion without operator action.

behaviors.description: For a returning patient case, the full live intake pipeline uses wider context (for example prior chart/history and cross-step state) so downstream decisions remain consistent with earlier triage and scheduling outputs, without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model behavior using richer context and on LLM-involved handoff consistency across stages. Rejected Workflow because behavior still describes one model decision point (triage) rather than multiple distinct agentic steps.
behaviors.test_description: Run live returning-patient intake with prior history context enabled; assert triage output reflects contextual factors and downstream scheduling/confirmation remain consistent with that triage outcome end-to-end.
